question_text: A book is resting on a table. Which of the following is true about the forces acting on it?

options.label: A
options.text: The forces are unbalanced, so the book will move

options.label: B
options.text: The forces are balanced, so the book remains at rest

options.label: C
options.text: There is only one force acting on the book

options.label: D
options.text: The weight of the book is greater than the normal force

correct_answer: The forces are balanced, so the book remains at rest
explanation: When a book rests on a table, two forces act on it: its weight (downward) and the normal force from the table (upward). These forces are equal in size but opposite in direction, so they are balanced. Balanced forces mean the book has no net force acting on it, so it stays at rest. This demonstrates Newton's first law of motion.
